title: padus australis
intro: Padus australis, also known as the Australian cherry or black alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Australia, belonging to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Padus australis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Padus australis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
